topic: An employee finds out a junior colleague makes more money
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elias stared at the spreadsheet, the numbers swimming before his eyes. He’d been meticulously tracking departmental budgets for the past quarter, a task he usually approached with the focused intensity of a surgeon. Then he saw it: Sarah’s compensation package. A sudden, cold wave washed over him, making his hands tremble slightly as he reread the figures. He blinked several times, trying to force the information to make sense, but it just remained stubbornly out of reach. He leaned back in his chair, the squeak of the worn leather echoing loudly in the quiet office. "Wait," he mumbled, his voice sounding thin. He quickly checked the source data, the confirmation hitting him like a physical blow. He felt a strange pressure behind his sternum, a complete inability to process the discrepancy. He slowly closed the file, his gaze fixed on a meaningless spot on the wall, his usual composure completely shattered.

[story 2]

"Seriously?" Mark managed to croak out, the sound barely audible above the hum of the server room. He looked across the cubicle partition at Ben, whose smug, easy smile seemed utterly unconcerned. Mark felt a sudden, dizzying lurch in his stomach. He’d spent the last hour cross-referencing salary bands, and the reality presented itself in stark, undeniable contrast. His jaw tightened involuntarily. He picked up his coffee mug, but his fingers fumbled, causing the liquid to slosh dangerously close to the rim. He took a deep, shaky breath, trying to anchor himself, but the air felt too thick to draw in. He shook his head once, a slow, jerky motion, as if trying to dislodge a faulty perception. The numbers, once abstract data points, now felt like a physical weight pressing down on his chest.

[story 3]

I watched the screen flicker, the final calculation resolving itself into a figure that made my breath catch. It was impossible. I leaned closer to my monitor, my knuckles whitening as I gripped the edge of the desk. Every line item, every bonus structure, seemed to rearrange itself into a completely different reality. My mouth went dry. I swallowed hard, the sensation catching in my throat, and I couldn't seem to form a coherent thought. I slowly pushed my chair back, the scrape of the wheels on the linoleum floor sounding deafening in the silence. I stood up, pacing three short steps before stopping abruptly, my eyes wide and unfocused. I rubbed my temples vigorously, trying to scrub the confusing data from my mind, but it just spun faster, a relentless, dizzying vortex.
